title: alisma plantago-aquatica var. parviflorum
intro: Alisma plantago-aquatica var. parviflorum, also known as the small-flowered water plantain, is a perennial aquatic plant species belonging to the Alismataceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as edema and joint pain. The health benefits of Alisma plantago-aquatica var. parviflorum include its potential to aid in the treatment of conditions related to liver and kidney function, as well as its ability to reduce blood sugar levels and improve circulation. Alisma plantago-aquatica var. parviflorum can be prepared into various herbal forms, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
